[[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:pages from wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]]

Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]]

[[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto 健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto 健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kengun Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kengun Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kengun Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kengun Shrine}} [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:goodbye $1]][[category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kengun Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kengun Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kengun Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kengun Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kengun Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kengun Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto
