[[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding 健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Kengun Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[en:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kengun Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]][[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Kengun Shrine}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[sn:Kengun Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Kengun Shrine}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[sn:Kengun Shrine]]

健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Kengun Shrine}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[sn:Kengun Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[Emperor Keikō]] or [[Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[Kokushi (official)]] of [[Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] or [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|05|N|130|45|19|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi (official)]] of [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] or [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[:en:Kumamoto Castle|Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[:en:Katō Shrine|Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[:en:Kumamoto Castle|Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[:en:Katō Shrine|Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q167136]] Wikidata name: Kengun Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q167136]] Wikidata name: Kengun Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = File:Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.78472222|N|130.75527778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi (official)]] of [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] or [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Higashi-ku|qid=Q875464}} }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[:en:Kumamoto City|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[:en:Kumamoto Castle|Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[:en:Katō Shrine|Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[sn:Kengun Shrine]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[:en:Kumamoto Castle|Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[:en:Katō Shrine|Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q167136}}

健軍神社 | image = File:Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.78472222|N|130.75527778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi (official)]] of [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] or [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Higashi-ku|WD=Q875464}} }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[:en:Kumamoto City|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[:en:Kumamoto Castle|Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[:en:Katō Shrine|Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q167136}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q167136}} [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Kengun Shrine]][[fr:Kengun-jinja]][[ja:健軍神社]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[fr:Kengun-jinja]][[ja:健軍神社]] [[en:Kengun Shrine]][[fr:Kengun-jinja]][[ja:健軍神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q167136]] Wikidata name: Kengun Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q167136]] Wikidata name: Kengun Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kengun Shrine
健軍神社 | image = File:Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.78472222|N|130.75527778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi (official)]] of [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto]] | established = the era of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] or [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Higashi-ku|WD=Q875464}} }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[:en:Kumamoto City|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]s decided to side with the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the [[:en:Kumamoto Castle|Kumamoto Castle]]. At the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], the [[kami]] of burnt [[:en:Katō Shrine|Katō Shrine]] and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *[[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q167136}} [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

健軍神社 | image = File:Kengunjinjya-shrine premises 1.jpg | caption = Kengun Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.78472222|N|130.75527778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Prefectural Shrine | deity = 健緒組命(takeogunino-mikoto),健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others | founded_by = {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi (official)}} of {{ill|Kumamoto|en|Kumamoto|WD=Q199889|lt=Kumamoto}} | established = the era of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} or {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} | date_destroyed = | location = 13-1 Kengun Honmachi, Higashi-ku, Kumamoto-shi | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Higashi-ku|WD=Q875464}} }} '''Kengun Shrine (健軍神社)''' is the oldest traditional shrine in [[:en:Kumamoto City|Kumamoto City]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of the 4 shrines of [[Aso Shrine]] group ([[Aso Shrine]], Kohsa Shrine and Kohnoura Shrine). ==History== ===Legend=== *According to a legend, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} ordered ''健緒組命'' (''Takeogumino-mikoto'') to conquer enemies who were around Mt. Choraina or Chorainasan (at Mashiki ?). He succeeded and was enshrined. (Local [[kami]] or shrine called ubugamisama might be there) *According to an old document, this shrine was built in the 19th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} (欽明天皇 ''Kinmei-tennō'', 509–571), the 29th emperor of Japan. A governor of Higo Province, now [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]] was on his way to [[Aso Shrine]] to pray for his country, but heavy snow prevented him from going to Aso. A boy, about 3-years of age, appeared to him and said, "Your faith in Aso God is very deep, and you are going to Aso in spite of heavy snow. You should build a shrine here, since [[Aso Shrine]] was busy with the defense of the emperor who is east, while you must defend this place against enemies west, so the name should be Kengun.Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine, on Dec. 11, 2010 ===13 kami are enshrined=== *The main [[kami]] is Kengun Ohkami(Take-ogumino-mikoto), who was a related [[kami]] of [[Aso Shrine]]. The name of kami is 健緒組命 and 健 is also called Ken.　Other [[kami]] are 健磐龍命,阿蘇都比賣命 and others. They came by [[Bunrei]] from [[Aso Shrine]]. ===Satsuma Rebellion=== *On February 22, 1877, 1400 {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}}s decided to side with the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}} force in the Kengun Shrine area and formed a Kumamoto Company and they started to fight against the {{ill|Kumamoto Castle|en|Kumamoto Castle|WD=Q613355|lt=Kumamoto Castle}}. At the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, the [[kami]] of burnt {{ill|Katō Shrine|en|Katō Shrine|WD=Q137707|lt=Katō Shrine}} and [[kami]] of burnt [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] were transiently relocated at the Kengun Shrine. ==Small Shrines and Road to the Shrine== *Amemiya Shrine:Amemiya Ohkami is enshrined. Here a ceremony is held for rainfall. *Miwa Shrine:Ohmononushi-Ohkami and others are enshrined for the prevention and cure of smallpox. People touch a stone there with depressions resembling smallpox scars and will gain some improvement or profit. *Kokuzou Shrine:A relocated shrine due to the construction of the streetcar road. Its origin is not available. *Hiyoshi Shrine:Here they pray for the prevention of fires. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Tensha Shrine:Tensha Ohkami is enshrined. A relocated shrine due to the city planning law. *Hatchou-baba:800 meter-long road to the Shrine. There is a [[torii]] at the front. ==Cultural Properties of Japan== *Higo [[Kagura]] or local Shinto theatrical dance: (prefectural properties) *{{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}} road (800 meters long) for horse riding :(city properties) *Kengun Shrine area :(city properties) ==References== *Pamphlet of the Kengun Shrine on Dec. 11, 2010. *[http://mirahouse.jp/landscape/kuma/shrine_kengun/index.html Kengun Shrine] *[http://www.infobears.ne.jp/athome/fukusuke291/z-807kengunjinjya.htm Kengun Shrine photograph] ==Footnotes== ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Authority control}} {{Aso Faith}} [[en:Kengun Shrine]] [[fr:Kengun-jinja]] [[ja:健軍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q167136}} [[Category:558 establishments]] [[Category:6th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Aso shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
